sections.paragraph:
εὐαρεστέω (s. ἀρεστός and εὐάρεστος) fut. εὐαρεστήσω Ps 114:9; 1 aor. εὐηρέστησα; pf. εὐηρέστηκα; on the augment s. B-D-F §69, 4.

① to do someth. or act in a manner that is pleasing or satisfactory, please, be pleasing τινί to someone (SIG 611, 19 [189 b.c.]; Diod S 14, 4, 2; Plut., Mor. 121f; Epict. 1, 12, 8; 2, 23, 42; LXX; Just.; Tat. 9:2) τῷ θεῷ (Gen 6:9; Philo, Abr. 35, Exsecr. 167; cp. TestGad 7:6) 1 Cl 41:1; Hv 3, 1, 9. Of Enoch, who pleased God (Gen 5:22, 24; Sir 44:16) Hb 11:5 (PKatz, Kratylos 5, ’60, 160f), and in the same context vs. 6, where the verb stands abs.; τῷ παντοκράτορι θεῷ ὁσίως εὐ. 1 Cl 62:2. Of Jesus εὐηρέστησεν τῷ πέμψαντι αὐτόν IMg 8:2; please the Lord (Christ) Pol 5:2.

② to experience pleasure, be pleased, take delight

ⓐ act. (Lysippus Com. [V b.c.] 7 ed. Kock I 702) τινί with or in someth. (Dionys. Hal. 11, 60, 1; EpArist 286; cp. Ps 25:3) τῷ νόμῳ with the law Hs 8, 3, 5 (but here mng. 1 cannot be totally ruled out).

ⓑ the pass. is mostly used in this sense (prob. POxy 265, 43 [I a.d.] καὶ εὐαρ[εστοῦμαι?]) εὐαρεστοῦμαί τινι be satisfied w. someth. (Diod S 3, 55, 9; 20, 79, 2; Diog. L. 10, 137). τοιαύταις θυσίαις with such sacrifices Hb 13:16.—DELG s.v. ἀρέσκω. M-M. TW.

fut. fut. = fut.

aor. aor. = aorist

  1. pf. = perfect

B-D-F B-D-F = FBlass, ADebrunner, RFunk, A Greek Grammar of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature—List 6

someth. someth. = something

SIG SIG = Sylloge Inscriptionum Graecarum3; superscript omitted in text—List 3

Diod S Diod S , I b.c.—List 5

Plut Plut , I–II a.d.—List 5

Epict Epict , various works, I–II a.d.—List 5

LXX LXX = Septuaginta, ed. ARahlfs, unless otherwise specified—Lists 2, beg.

Just Just , II a.d.—List 5

Tat Tat , II a.d.—List 5

Philo Philo = P. of Alexandria, I b.c.–I a.d.—List 5

  1. cp. = compare, freq. in ref. to citation fr. ancient texts

TestGad TestGad = Testament of Gad, s. Test12Patr—List 2

1 Cl 1 Cl = 1 Clement—List 1

Hv Hv = Visions

abs. abs. = absolute

IMg IMg = Ignatius to the Magnesians—List 1

Pol Pol = Polycarp to the Philippians—List 1

act. act. = active

Lysippus Lysippus Com , s. Com.; V b.c.—List 5

  1. ed. = edited (by), edition

Kock Kock , s. Com.—List 5

EpArist EpArist = Epistle of Aristeas, II b.c.—List 5

Hs Hs = Similitudes

mng. mng. = meaning(s)

pass. pass. = passive (either of grammatical form or of passive experience); also used in reference to literary portion=passage

prob. prob. = probable, probably

POxy POxy = Oxyrhynchus Papyri—List 4

Diog. L. Diog. L. = Diogenes Laertius, III a.d.—List 5

DELG DELG = PChantraine, Dictionnaire étymologique de la langue grecque—List 6

s.v. s.v. = sub voce (under the word, look up the word)

M-M M-M = JMoulton/GMilligan, Vocabulary of Greek Testament—Lists 4, 6

TW TW = Theologisches Wörterbuch zum NT; tr. GBromiley, Theological Dictionary of the NT—List 6

Arndt, William ; Danker, Frederick W. ; Bauer, Walter: A Greek-English Lexicon of the New Testament and Other Early Christian Literature. 3rd ed. Chicago : University of Chicago Press, 2000, S. 403
